title: Share of local governments with disaster risk reduction strategies
subtitle: Share of local governments that have adopted and implemented local disaster risk reduction strategies in line with the national strategy.
caption:
Share of local governments with disaster risk reduction strategies

Share of local governments that have adopted and implemented local disaster risk reduction strategies in line with the national strategy.

This choropleth world map displays the share of local governments with disaster risk reduction strategies for the year 2024. The legend ranges from 0% to 90%, using a color scale that transitions from pale yellow for the lowest shares to dark green for the highest shares, with a specific hatched pattern indicating no data. The darkest green band, representing shares between 80% and 90%, covers the United States, Canada, Russia, China, and most of Western and Eastern Europe. In South America, Brazil and Argentina also appear in this highest visible band. Australia and New Zealand sit in a slightly lighter dark green band, corresponding to the 70% to 80% range. A distinct cluster of countries in North and West Africa, including Algeria, Libya, and Mauritania, along with Saudi Arabia and India, appear in pale yellow shades, placing them in the lowest bands between 0% and 20%. Southern Africa shows variation, with South Africa in a mid-range green band around 40% to 50%, while Namibia and Botswana appear in lighter shades. Large portions of the map, including Greenland, much of Central Africa, and parts of the Middle East and Central Asia, are covered in diagonal hatching, indicating that no data is available for these regions. The visual distribution highlights high adoption rates across North America, Europe, and parts of Asia, contrasted with lower reported shares in parts of Africa and the Middle East, alongside significant gaps in data coverage globally.
